title: Tremor motion quantifying method and system
abstract: There is disclosed a method of quantifying tremor of a subject; attaching at least one sensor on each of the two adjacent segments on the subject body; acquiring data from the sensors; whereby the multiplanar relative movements of the two adjacent hand- arm segments are captured; computing the multiplanar relative motions of the two adjacent segments to obtain a joint angle in each motion; subjecting the joint angle to a filtering process to obtain a joint angular displacement; and quantifying the tremor motion of the multiplanar relative movements of two adjacent segments using the joint angular displacement.
claims:
Claims A method of quantifying tremor motion in a subject comprising: attaching at least one sensor on each of the two adjacent segments on the subject body; acquiring data from the sensors; whereby the multiplanar relative movements of the two adjacent segments are captured; computing the multiplanar relative motions of the two adjacent segments to obtain a joint angle; subjecting the joint angle to a filtering process to obtain a joint angular displacement; and quantifying the tremor motion of the multiplanar relative movements of two adjacent segments using the joint angular displacement. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the method further includes using the joint angular displacement for any tremor analysis process. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the sensor data is in the form of quaternion data. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the tremor motions include, but are not limited to flexion-extension, abduction-adduction and pronation-supination. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the method is used for multiple-degrees of relative movements. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the joint angular displacement is calculated based on an algorithm. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the filtering process is for removing motions not related to human tremor. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the filtering process is performed by a 

 bandpass filter having a passband of 3-30 Hz. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein the filtering process is performed to remove the large non-oscillatory content of the tremor data, which are typically caused by intended motion and some other unintended large motions; as well as signal noise. The method as claimed in Claim 1 , wherein computing relative motions include: assigning motion, M and indicator, / axes of the associated motions; determining the unit vectors of said motion, M and indicator, / axes, calculating a correction of quaternion rotation, q c , correcting the distal indicator axes, IDISTAU based on the computed quaternion rotation correction, and computing the angles between the corrected distal indicator axes, 1 oiSTALLcorrect an d original proximal indicator axes, IpROXi of each joint segment. 1 1. The method as claimed in Claim 10, wherein the motion, and indicator, / axes are defined such that: the axes within which a joint angle is obtained are termed indicator axes, whereas the axes about which individual motions rotate are the motion axes. 12. The method as claimed in Claim 3, wherein acquiring quaternion data includes: detecting the motion for each segment using sensing elements and acquire data from said sensing elements to obtain the quaternion data. The method as claimed in Claim 10, determining the unit vectors of the motion and indicator axes for any joint motion can be obtained using the following equation: p' = qpq 1 wherein; p is any three-dimensional vector; p' is the new vector rotated by the sensing element quaternion q is the quaternion for the sensing element q 1 is the inverse or the conjugate of the sensing element quaternion 14. The method as claimed in Claim 10, wherein the correction of the quaternion rotation, qc is computed by making the distal motion axes, M DISTALI to align with the proximal motion axis, M PROXI . 15. The method as claimed in Claim 10, wherein calculating the correction of the quaternion rotation, q c , for the purpose of positioning the distal indicator axes IDISTAL I to be on the same plane as the proximal indicator axes IpRoxi- 16. The method as claimed in Claim 10, wherein correcting the distal indicator axes, IDISTAL I based on the computed quaternion rotation correction, q c to obtain the corrected indicator axis of the distal sensing element, 17. The method as claimed in Claim 10, wherein computing angles between the corrected distal indicator axis, 1 oiSTALi_correct m d original proximal indicator axes, IpRoxi will result in the attainment of the joint angle. 18. A method of quantifying hand-arm tremor in a subject; the method comprising: attaching at least one sensor on each of the two adjacent segments of the hand- arm on the subject's body; whereby the multiplanar relative movements of the two adjacent hand-arm segments are captured; computing the multiplanar relative motions of the two adjacent hand-arm segments to obtain wrist and elbow joint angles; subjecting the wrist and elbow joint angles to a filtering process to obtain respective joint angular displacement; and quantifying the hand-arm tremor motion of the multiplanar relative movements of two adjacent segments using the joint angular displacement. wherein computing relative motions include: obtaining motion, and indicator, I axes of the associated motions; determining the unit vectors of said motion, M and indicator / axes, calculating a correction of quaternion rotation, correcting the distal indicator axes based on the computed quaternion rotation correction, and computing the angles between the corrected distal indicator axes 1 oiSTALtcorrect an d original proximal indicator axes Ipjtoxi- of each joint segment. 19. The method as claimed in Claim 18, wherein the method of quantifying hand- arm tremor further includes: detecting motions of the distal end of the upper 

 arm and sending signals based on the detection; detecting motions of a distal end of the lower arm and sending signals based on the detection; and detecting the motions of a dorsum portion of the hand and sending signals based on the detection. The method as claimed in Claim 18, wherein classifying the detected motions of the hand-arm tremor includes: classifying the detected motions into four motions i.e. wrist flexionâ extension (WFE), wrist abduction-adduction (WAA), elbow flexion-extension (EFE) and elbow pronationâ supination (Eps). A system for quantifying at least one joint tremor(s) motion of a subject; the system comprising: at least one kinetic sensor is positioned on each of the two adjacent segments on the subject's body; means for acquiring data from at least one of the sensors; whereby the multiplanar relative movements of the two adjacent hand-arm segments are captured; means for computing the multiplanar relative motions of the two adjacent segments to obtain a joint angle in each motion; means for quantifying the tremor motion of the multiplanar relative movements of two adjacent segments using the joint angular displacement.
